out_of_scope_note: Wearable/clinical signal-processing paper: applies Bayesian filtering within an EM framework as a state-space estimator to recover unobserved sympathetic arousal and cortisol-related energy from skin conductance (marked point process) and cortisol/heart-rate observations, with validation against fNIRS features. Aimed at wearable monitoring and closed-loop therapy delivery for neuropsychiatric/hormone disorders. Bayesian filtering is used here as a peripheral-physiology estimation tool, not as a theoretical framework about neural computation, perception, decision-making, or encoding/decoding. No neural recordings, no circuit model, no cognitive computation — out of scope for the lab's perceptual/Bayesian-observer/efficient-coding research.
